folio.label: 36r
folio.l: And haddest gold I now , and Emelye
¢ Noman myght gladen , Theseus
Sauyng his old fader , Egeus
That knew this worldlis , tnsmutaciou~
As he had seyn it chaunge , boæe vp & doun~
Ioye after woo , and woo after gladnes
And shewed hi~ ensample , and lyknes
¢ Rigút as there deyed neuäe man , q¶ he

note.notetxt: 1979 There is a No after the paragraph mark.Est

folio.l: That he ne loued in erthe , in som degre
Rigút so æere lyued neuäe man , he seide
In al this world , æ somtyme he ne deyede
This world nys but a thurgú feire , ful of woo
And we ben pilgémes , passyng to and froo
Deth is an ende , of euäy worldly sore
And ouä al this , ©et seide he meche more
To this effect , ful wysly to enhorte
The peeple , æ they shuld hi~ , recomforte
¢ Duke Theseus , with al his bysy cure
Cast now where , that the sepulture
Of goode Arcite , mymaymay best I maked be
And eke most honoåable , in his degre
And at the last , he tok conclusiou~
That there as first , Arcite & Palamou~
Hadden for loue , the bataylle he~ bytwene
That in the selue Groue , swete & grene
There as he had , his amorous desires
Hys compleynt , and for loue , his hote fires
He wold make a fir , in which the office
FuneraÂ , he mygút al accomplice
And lete a non comaunde , to hakke and hewe
The Okes olde , and leyn hem on a rewe
In culpons , wel arayed for to brenne
¢ His officers , w swift fet they renne
And ride a nonö , at his comandement
And after this , Theseus hath I sent
After a Bere , and it al ouä spredde
W clothes of gold , the richest æ he hadde
And of æe same seute , he clad Arcite
Vp on his handes , his glowys whyte
Eke on his hede , a corone of laurere grene
And in his hand , a swerd ful brigút and kene
He leid hi~ bare , the visage , on the bere
Ther w he wepte , that pite was to here
And for the peeple , shuld sen hi~ alle
Whan it was day , he brou©t hi~ to æe halle
Thax roreth of the criyng and the soun~
